text.title: Crossplane Composition for Self-Service Infra Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every abstraction MUST be published as an XRD with a documented claim shape; ad-hoc Composition without XRD is rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Compositions MUST embed security defaults (encryption, IAM least privilege, backup retention) — not optional overrides.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Every claim MUST cap the resource size (CPU / memory / storage / instance count) below a documented threshold.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Crossplane ProviderConfigs MUST use short-lived credentials (IRSA / Workload Identity); long-lived keys rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: XRDs + Compositions MUST be deployed via GitOps (ArgoCD / Flux); kubectl apply by hand rejected.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.

rule.statement: Skip when Terraform-only stack OR <5 abstractions justify Crossplane overhead.
rule.rationale: Operational rationale tying the rule to the cited source.
